title: The people behind the papers - Valentyna Kostiuk and Mustafa Khokha.
abstract: The nuclear pore has been thought to have a consistent function during development, although its composition can vary. In this issue, Valentyna Kostiuk and colleagues reveal a context-specific role of a nucleoporin, Nup107, in the maternal-to-zygotic transition. We caught up with first author Valentyna Kostiuk and corresponding author Mustafa Khokha, Professor and Principal Investigator at Yale School of Medicine, USA, to learn more.
